para_trans: Implementation of the provisions of the Government Circular dated 2.6.2025
About.....
Government of Maharashtra
Revenue and Forest Department
Government Circular No: Narrow 2026 / Q.E.1550473 / Rehabilitation-1
First Floor, Madam Cama Marg, Martyrdom Rajguru Chowk,
Mantralaya, Mumbai - 400032.
Date: April 7, 2026
Government Circular No: Narrow 2026 / Q.E.1550473 / Rehabilitation-1
Reference: 1) Government Circular No. RPA / 2024 / Q. 75/2-1, dated 2nd June 2025
2) Government Letter No. RPA-2025 / E-Office 1009352 / R-1, dated 4th March 2025
Preamble: - Section 6 of the Hindu Succession Rights (Amendment) Act, 2005, Rehabilitation Act
Section 2 (2) (a) and the proviso thereto shall be construed together with the scientific meaning, instead of "date of prohibition."

Consistency and clarity in granting rehabilitation benefits to the heirs of landholders who died before the project moratorium date as the criterion of "date of award of acquired land" is more compoundable.
For the purpose of introduction, the Government Gazette dated 2nd June 2025 has been issued. Sadar
Rehabilitation benefits to be paid to the project affected heirs as a separate entity / unit based on the provisions of the circular.
This is the clear objective of the government.
However, some District Rehabilitation Officers based on a letter dated 4.3.2025 to the heirs

Independent projects are refusing to offer rehabilitation benefits as aggrieved account holders. Since the matter is completely inconsistent with the policy prescribed under the Government Circular dated 2.6.2025, the heirs of deceased landholders shall be entitled to
The following clarificatory instructions are being given regarding the payment of benefits: -
Government Circular: -

1. The provisions of the Government Circular dated 2nd June 2025 on "Grant of Rehabilitation Benefits to the heirs of Landholders who have died before the ban date of the project" are binding in all cases.
Action should be taken accordingly.
Since the letter dated 4.3.2025 was issued before the Government Circular dated 2.6.2025, the directions in this letter are inconsistent with the provisions of the Government Circular dated 2.6.2025.
They will be superfluous and ineffective.
3. Guideline in Government Circular dated 2.6.2025, while interpreting the term "joint family doer."
According to the principles, each son / daughter of the original landholder (and their next-generation heirs) shall be entitled to
The project should be treated as a separate entity or a unit.

4. The concept of "Jointly one unit" in the earlier Government Letters / Circulars is limited to the reference to allocation of land acquisition award amount; it is the status of project affected person under the Rehabilitation Act.
Not applicable for determination.
5. Pending by all District Rehabilitation Officers, Sub-Divisional Officers as well as Revenue Officers concerned.
Review of cases, separate to all heirs eligible under Circular Date 2.6.2025
Necessary review of grant of project affected status as component / unit and revised orders should be issued immediately.
6. Guidelines under Government Circular dated 2.6.2025 in all future cases.
Strict adherence to the principles. If any provision in the earlier letter is inconsistent, it should be ignored.
7. Remove confusion caused by typographical errors in the Government Letter dated 4.3.2025

To do this, the words and brackets mentioned in the said definition must be grayed out according to the wording contained in the original act. If the benefit is denied to the eligible heirs only on the basis of typographical inaccuracy, the
The action will be inconsistent.
8. All Collectors to inform the Government that these instructions are being strictly implemented.
Report should be submitted. The Divisional Commissioner has reviewed the issue and is complying with the directions.
Be careful.
This government circular is available on the website of the Government of Maharashtra, www.maharashtra.gov.in.
